segments.text: Why doesn't the Almighty set a definite time to punish the wicked? Why do those who follow him never see him act in judgment?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The wicked move boundary stones;  they seize other people's flocks and move them to their own pastures.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They steal the orphan's donkey; they take the widow's ox as security for a debt.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They push the poor out of their way; the destitute are forced to hide from them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Like wild donkeys in the desert, the poor have to scavenge for their food, looking for anything to feed their children in the wasteland.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are forced to find what they can in other people's fields, to glean among the vineyards of the wicked.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They spend the night naked because they have no clothes; they have nothing to cover themselves against the cold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are soaked by the cold mountain storms, and huddle beside the rocks for shelter.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Fatherless children are snatched from their mother's breasts, taking the babies of the poor as security for a debt.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Because they have no clothes to wear they have to go naked, harvesting sheaves of grain while they themselves are hungry.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the olive groves they work to produce oil, but do not taste it; they tread the winepress, but are thirsty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the city the dying groan, and the wounded cry for help, but God ignores their prayers.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: These are people who rebel against the light. They do not want to know its ways, or to stay on its paths.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The murderer gets up at dawn to kill the poor and needy, and when night falls he becomes a thief.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The adulterer waits for dusk, saying to himself, ‘No one will see me now,’ and he covers his face.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Thieves break into houses during the night and they sleep during the day. They don't even know what the light is like!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Total darkness is like light to them, for they are familiar with the night.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Like bubbles on the surface of a river they are quickly carried away. The land they own is cursed by God. They don't enter their own vineyards.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Just as heat and drought dry up snowmelt, so Sheol takes away those who have sinned.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even their mothers forget them, maggots feast on them, they are no longer remembered, and their wickedness becomes like a tree that is broken into pieces.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They mistreat childless women and are mean to widows.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God prolongs the life of the wicked by his power; but when they arise, they have no assurance of life.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He supports them and gives them security, but he is always watching what they're doing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Though they may be illustrious for a while, soon they are gone. They are brought down like all others, cut off like the heads of grain.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If this isn't so, who can prove I'm a liar and there's nothing to what I say?
segments.speaker: Job
